requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'AdaServe: SLO-Customized LLM Serving with Fine-Grained Speculative Decoding' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The infrastructure, models, and datasets required for the evaluation are available.
Code has been implemented to access and load the Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct model with 4-way tensor parallelism on 4 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPUs.

verification.spec: Inspect the model-loading/config code and verify (a) it references meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct, (b) sets tensor_parallel_size=4 (or equivalent FlexFlow Serve 4-way TP config), and (c) the runtime initializes successfully on 4 A100 80GB GPUs (check torch.cuda.device_count()==4 and successful weight load).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to access and load the Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct model with 2-way tensor parallelism on 2 NVIDIA A100 80GB GPUs.

verification.spec: Inspect model-loading code and verify it references Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct with tensor_parallel_size=2, and that the runtime initializes successfully on 2 A100 80GB GPUs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to access and load the Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct model as the draft model for Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.

verification.spec: Inspect draft-model config and verify it references meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct, that the model is loaded colocated on a GPU with the target model, and that a forward pass succeeds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to access and load the Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct model as the draft model for Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct.

verification.spec: Inspect draft-model config and verify it references Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct, model loads colocated with the target Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct, and a forward pass succeeds.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to colocate the draft model with the base model on one of the GPUs.

verification.spec: Inspect the model placement configuration. With tensor parallelism across N GPUs for the base model, confirm that the draft model is loaded onto one of those same GPUs (e.g., device_map includes the draft model on GPU 0) rather than a separate dedicated device.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the HumanEval dataset (164 programming problems) for the coding copilot category.

verification.spec: Run the workload-loader for category 1 and confirm it returns exactly 164 prompts taken from openai_humaneval. Check each example contains the HumanEval 'prompt' field with a Python function signature/docstring.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the Alpaca dataset (52k instruction-following examples) for the chatbot category.

verification.spec: Run the workload-loader for category 2 and confirm it returns ~52000 instruction-following examples from the Alpaca dataset (tatsu-lab/alpaca). Verify each example contains 'instruction' and 'output' fields.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the CNN/DailyMail dataset for the summarization category.

verification.spec: Run the workload-loader for category 3 and confirm it returns examples from CNN/DailyMail (cnn_dailymail) with 'article' and 'highlights' fields. Verify the prompts framed for summarization include the article text.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to access the real-world trace timestamps used to generate evaluation traces (per Figure 7).

verification.spec: Load the real-world trace timestamp file referenced by the paper (the trace plotted in Figure 7, originating from prior work [39]). Confirm the loader returns a list/array of arrival timestamps that can be truncated and rescaled to target RPS values, matching the bursty pattern shown in Figure 7.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to integrate the batched prefill kernel from FlashInfer for both speculation steps and LLM verification.

verification.spec: Use importlib and grep to confirm the codebase imports flashinfer (e.g., 'import flashinfer' or 'from flashinfer') and that the batched prefill kernel is invoked both in the speculation step path and the LLM verification path.

sub_tasks.requirements: The problem formulation and optimal token tree construction algorithm (Algorithm 1) have been implemented.
Code has been implemented to compute A(r_i) = (l_i + t_spec) / t_i^TPOT - o_i, the minimum number of tokens that must be accepted for request r_i to attain its TPOT SLO.

verification.spec: Unit-test the A(r_i) computation function: pass synthetic values for l_i, t_spec, t_i^TPOT, o_i and assert the returned value equals (l_i + t_spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i to within float tolerance. Test multiple parameter combinations including edge cases where A(r_i) is negative or large.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to maintain the budget constraint such that the total number of nodes across all token trees does not exceed the hardware budget B.

verification.spec: Run the full SLO-customized selection + throughput-optimized selection pipeline with a known budget B over n requests. Sum |T_i| across all returned trees and assert the total <= B in every iteration. Test multiple (B, n) combinations.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute the path probability f(v) of a node v as the product of conditional probabilities along the path from the root.

verification.spec: Construct a small candidate tree with manually specified conditional probabilities M_q(v | parent). Call the path-probability computation function and assert f(v) equals the product of conditional probabilities along the path from root to v, for every node. Validate to within 1e-6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for Step 1 of Algorithm 1: iteratively adding the highest-f(v) node from each request's tree until n_acc[i] >= A(r_i), returning INVALID if budget is exhausted.

verification.spec: Construct a unit test with n=2 toy requests, a synthetic infinite tree per request providing f(v) values (mock), A(r_1)=0.6, A(r_2)=0.8, and budget B. Invoke the Step-1 routine. Verify: (a) at each iteration the node added has the max f(v) over remaining candidates; (b) iteration stops for request i once n_acc[i] >= A(r_i); (c) returns INVALID when B is exhausted before all SLOs are met. Test the INVALID path explicitly with a tight B.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for Step 2 of Algorithm 1: allocating remaining budget to insert additional high-f(v) nodes from the union of all infinite token trees.

verification.spec: Unit test Step 2: with mock infinite trees for n requests and remaining budget B', verify the routine repeatedly selects GetTop over the union of all (T_inf - S_added) and assigns each chosen node to its source request's tree until B'=0. Verify total nodes added equals B'; nodes have non-increasing f(v) order; no node is duplicated.

sub_tasks.requirements: The AdaServe system architecture has been implemented with request manager and execution engine components.
Code has been implemented for the request manager containing a request pool and SLO-customized scheduler that runs SLO-customized speculative decoding.

verification.spec: Import the request manager module and assert it exposes a request pool data structure (e.g., a class with add/get methods) and an SLO-customized scheduler class whose step method runs the full speculate-select-verify pipeline.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for the execution engine responsible for executing both the draft and target models on GPUs.

verification.spec: Import the execution engine module and confirm it exposes interfaces for running both the draft (speculator) model and the target (verifier) LLM on GPU. Verify the engine class has methods such as run_draft and run_verify (or equivalent) that dispatch to GPU.

sub_tasks.requirements: Baselines have been set up for comparison.
vLLM (v0.8.4) has been set up with PagedAttention and continuous batching for use as a baseline.

verification.spec: Verify that vLLM v0.8.4 is installed (import vllm; check vllm.__version__ == '0.8.4'). Launch a vLLM engine with default settings on the target model and submit a small batch of requests; confirm PagedAttention is in use (check engine config) and that continuous batching processes them successfully.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM augmented with priority scheduling (vLLM + Priority) has been set up to allow urgent requests to preempt non-urgent ones during decoding.

verification.spec: Submit a mixed batch with high-priority (urgent) and low-priority requests to the vLLM+Priority baseline; verify via logs/trace that high-priority requests preempt low-priority ones during decoding (i.e., low-priority requests are paused or evicted while high-priority requests are scheduled to run) and that high-priority requests achieve lower TPOT than low-priority ones under contention.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM + Chunked Prefill baseline has been set up.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with chunked-prefill enabled (e.g., --enable-chunked-prefill) and submit a request with a long prompt; verify via execution trace that prefill is split into multiple chunks across iterations rather than executed as a single forward pass, and that decoding tokens from other requests can be batched with prefill chunks.

sub_tasks.requirements: Sarathi-Serve has been set up with chunked prefill for jointly batching prefill and decoding stages.

verification.spec: Run Sarathi-Serve on a workload mixing long-prompt and decoding-phase requests; verify via logs/scheduler trace that prefill is split into chunks and that each iteration's batch jointly contains prefill chunks and decode tokens from different requests (stall-free scheduling).

sub_tasks.requirements: FastServe baseline (preemptive scheduling) has been set up.

verification.spec: Run FastServe on a workload containing both short and long-output requests; verify via the scheduler log/trace that long-running requests are preempted (e.g., demoted to lower-priority queue using multi-level feedback queue) so short requests can complete first, characteristic of FastServe's preemptive scheduling.

sub_tasks.requirements: VTC (Virtual Token Counter) baseline for fair scheduling based on tokens processed has been set up.

verification.spec: Submit requests from multiple clients/services with skewed load to the VTC baseline; verify via scheduler trace/logs that the system tracks per-client token counts and prioritizes the client with the fewest processed tokens (fair scheduling), e.g., the lagging client's requests are scheduled ahead of the heavier client's.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM-Spec(4) baseline with sequence-based speculative decoding using 4 speculated tokens has been set up.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with sequence-based speculative decoding configured with num_speculative_tokens=4 and a draft model from the same family as the target. Issue a sample request and confirm via logs/metrics that exactly 4 tokens are speculated per step.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM-Spec(6) baseline with sequence-based speculative decoding using 6 speculated tokens has been set up.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with sequence-based speculative decoding configured with num_speculative_tokens=6. Issue a sample request and confirm via logs/metrics that 6 tokens are speculated per step.

sub_tasks.requirements: vLLM-Spec(8) baseline with sequence-based speculative decoding using 8 speculated tokens has been set up.

verification.spec: Launch vLLM with sequence-based speculative decoding configured with num_speculative_tokens=8. Issue a sample request and confirm via logs/metrics that 8 tokens are speculated per step.

sub_tasks.requirements: The workload generation and SLO configuration have been set up correctly.
Category 1 (coding copilot) requests are sourced from HumanEval and assigned a TPOT SLO of 1.2× the baseline latency.

verification.spec: Inspect the workload generator: for category-1 requests, confirm prompts are sampled from the HumanEval dataset (164 programming problems) and the assigned TPOT SLO equals 1.2 * measured_baseline_latency for the model under test, where baseline_latency is measured at near-zero load.

sub_tasks.requirements: Category 2 (chatbot) requests are sourced from Alpaca and assigned a TPOT SLO of 50ms per token.

verification.spec: Inspect the workload configuration file or constants that define request categories. Assert that the Category 2 (chatbot) entry references the Alpaca dataset (e.g., 'tatsu-lab/alpaca' or local path containing alpaca instructions) and that its TPOT SLO is set to exactly 50 ms/token (or 0.050 s).

sub_tasks.requirements: Category 3 (summarization) requests are sourced from CNN/DailyMail and assigned a TPOT SLO of 150ms per token.

verification.spec: Inspect the workload configuration file or constants that define request categories. Assert that the Category 3 (summarization) entry references the CNN/DailyMail dataset (e.g., 'cnn_dailymail' HF dataset identifier) and that its TPOT SLO is set to exactly 150 ms/token (or 0.150 s).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented so that for each arriving request, its category is sampled according to a specified probability distribution and then a request is sampled uniformly from the corresponding dataset.

verification.spec: Verify the request-sampling function: given a category distribution (e.g., 0.6/0.2/0.2) and the three datasets, generate 10000 requests. Check that empirical category proportions match within 2% and that within each category, sampled prompts are drawn uniformly (Chi-square test on a small subset).

sub_tasks.requirements: Evaluation metrics have been implemented correctly.
Code has been implemented to compute SLO attainment as the percentage of requests whose average per-token latency is no greater than the specified TPOT SLO threshold.

verification.spec: Import the SLO attainment computation function. Construct a synthetic set of completed requests with known per-token latencies and TPOT SLO thresholds (e.g., 5 requests with latencies [40, 45, 60, 55, 30] ms/token and SLO 50 ms/token). Call the function and assert it returns 3/5 = 60.0% (requests with avg per-token latency <= threshold). Verify behavior on edge cases: all requests pass returns 100%, all fail returns 0%, empty list handled gracefully.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute goodput as the number of tokens generated per second for requests that successfully attain their SLO.

verification.spec: Import the goodput computation function. Construct synthetic request records with known output token counts, per-token latencies, total wall-clock duration, and TPOT SLO. For example: 3 requests passing SLO produce 100, 200, 150 tokens over a 10s window; 2 requests failing SLO produce 500 tokens. Assert function returns (100+200+150)/10 = 45 tokens/s (only counting SLO-attaining requests). Verify that failing requests do not contribute to goodput.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute the mean number of accepted tokens per request per verification step as a speculation accuracy metric.

verification.spec: Import the speculation accuracy metric function. Construct synthetic verification trace data: e.g., for 2 requests across 3 verification steps, accepted token counts are [[3,2,4],[1,3,2]]. Assert the function returns the mean accepted tokens per request per verification step = (3+2+4+1+3+2)/(2*3) = 2.5. Confirm dimensions are averaged over both requests and verification steps as described in Figure 12.

sub_tasks.requirements: The Figure 1 motivational experiment and ablation/sensitivity studies have been reproduced.
Under the synthetic trace on Qwen2.5-32B, AdaServe achieves approximately 83.0% SLO attainment, the highest among all systems (paper_category=claim, verification.type=measured).

verification.spec: Run AdaServe on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (2xA100 80GB) with the synthetic trace; measure overall SLO attainment percentage and verify it is approximately 83.0% and the highest of all systems.

sub_tasks.requirements: A latency breakdown has been measured for AdaServe across scheduling, speculation, and verification phases, demonstrating CPU overhead of approximately 0.41% on Qwen-2.5 (paper_category=claim, verification.type=measured).

verification.spec: Run AdaServe on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (2xA100 80GB) under a representative workload; instrument per-iteration time spent on CPU scheduling/selection vs GPU speculation+verification. Compute CPU overhead fraction = CPU_time / total_iteration_time and verify approx 0.41%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Latency breakdown shows CPU overhead of approximately 0.31% on Llama-3.1 (paper_category=claim, verification.type=measured).

verification.spec: Run AdaServe on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (4xA100 80GB) under a representative workload; instrument CPU vs GPU time per iteration. Compute CPU overhead fraction and verify approx 0.31%.

Run AdaServe and all baselines (vLLM, Sarathi-Serve, vLLM-Spec(4/6/8)). Compute SLO attainment ratio (AdaServe / max(baseline)) at each point; assert max ratio >= 1.5 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe achieves up to 30% higher goodput than the best baseline on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct under changing application distribution.

verification.spec: On Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (4-way TP, 4xA100-80G) sweep urgent-request proportion {30,50,70,90}% at RPS=4.0. Compute max(AdaServe_goodput / best_baseline_goodput); assert >= 1.30 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe reduces SLO violations by up to 4.3x on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct under the changing-application-distribution experiment.

verification.spec: On Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct sweep urgent-request proportion {30,50,70,90}% at RPS=4.0. Compute (1 - SLO_attainment) violation rate for each system; compute max over sweep of best_baseline_violation / AdaServe_violation; assert >= 4.3 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe reduces SLO violations by up to 3.7x on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct under the changing-application-distribution experiment.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe achieves up to 4.61x lower SLO violation rate than the best baseline on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct in the SLO-scale sweep.

verification.spec: On Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct at RPS=4.0 with 60% urgent requests, sweep SLO scale in {1.6,1.4,1.2,1.0,0.8,0.6}. Compute max over sweep of best_baseline_violation / AdaServe_violation; assert >= 4.61 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe achieves up to 3.05x lower SLO violation rate than the best baseline on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct in the SLO-scale sweep.

verification.spec: On Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct at RPS=4.0 with 60% urgent requests, sweep SLO scale {1.6,1.4,1.2,1.0,0.8,0.6}. Compute max best_baseline_violation / AdaServe_violation; assert >= 3.05 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that AdaServe achieves up to 1.38x higher goodput than the best baseline under strict TPOT SLOs in the SLO-scale sweep.

verification.spec: Sweep SLO scale {1.6..0.6} at RPS=4.0 with 60% urgent requests on both Llama-3.1-70B and Qwen2.5-32B. Compute max(AdaServe goodput / best baseline goodput) across the sweep; assert >= 1.38 within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that continuous batching baselines (vLLM, Sarathi-Serve) drop below 40% SLO attainment when SLO scale falls below 1.0.

verification.spec: Run vLLM and Sarathi-Serve on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct at RPS=4.0 with 60% urgent requests at SLO scales {0.8, 0.6}. Measure overall SLO attainment percentage; assert both systems' SLO attainment < 40% (i.e., paper_value 40 with tolerance allowing observed <= 48%).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that mean accepted tokens per verification per request for AdaServe on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct is high at low RPS and decreases with RPS, as shown in Figure 12.

verification.spec: Run AdaServe on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct across RPS {2.6, 3.4, 4.2, 4.6}. Log per-iteration accepted-token counts and compute mean accepted tokens per request per verification step. Assert mean acc tokens at RPS=2.6 is approximately 6 and decreases monotonically (within tolerance) to approximately 3 at RPS=4.6, matching Figure 12 trend.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that mean accepted tokens per verification per request for AdaServe on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct follows the Figure 12 trend (high at low RPS, decreasing at high RPS).

sub_tasks.paper_unit: mean accepted tokens at low RPS

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that on the synthetic mixed-application trace, AdaServe attains 83.6% SLO attainment on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct and 83.0% on Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (Figure 14).

verification.spec: Replay the synthetic trace shown in Figure 13 (chat, coding, summarization arrivals over ~6 minutes) on AdaServe with both Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct and Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct. Compute overall SLO attainment; assert ~83.6% on Llama and ~83.0% on Qwen within tolerance.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the speculation phase of SLO-customized speculative decoding produces a candidate token tree of width approximately 229 nodes per request as referenced in the algorithm pseudocode figure.

verification.spec: Run the speculation phase Spec(M_q, requests, d, w) with paper-default d,w and assert that the total number of candidate nodes per request matches the referenced size with relative tolerance 0.2

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the inner while-loop in Algorithm 2 (throughput-optimized selection) operates with the budget loop iterating over the expected number of candidate nodes (~127) as referenced in the algorithm figure.

verification.spec: Instrument throughput-optimized selection and assert that under the default config the loop processes ~127 candidate nodes (tolerance 0.2)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the CPU scheduling overhead (speculation + selection scheduling on CPU) is measured and is at most ~0.41% of end-to-end iteration time on Llama3-70B, matching the reported overhead.

Code has been implemented such that Figure 3's qualitative claim holds: under speculative decoding, the mean number of tokens accepted per verification step is strictly greater than 1 (i.e. SD produces >1 token per LLM forward).

verification.spec: Run AdaServe with speculative decoding on a small workload (e.g. 32 requests from Alpaca) and measure mean accepted tokens per verification step. Assert measured value >= 2.0.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the draft-token-tree construction shown in Figure 4: tree-structured candidates rooted at the last generated token, where each path from root corresponds to a candidate continuation and the tree is verified by the LLM in parallel.

verification.spec: Construct a small draft token tree with known structure (e.g. 7 nodes, depth 3); call the AdaServe verification function; assert the returned per-node logits/acceptance vector has length equal to the number of tree nodes and that the tree topology (parent/child indices) is preserved through the verification call.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the budget constraint of Section 3: total nodes across all draft token trees in one decoding iteration must not exceed the configured token budget B.

verification.spec: Run SLO-customized scheduler on a synthetic batch of n=4 requests with budget B=16; inspect returned draft token trees and assert sum_i |T_i| <= B. Repeat for B in {8, 16, 32, 64}.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the TPOT constraint of Section 3: for each request r_i, the scheduler attempts to ensure E[acc(T_i)] >= A(r_i) where A(r_i) = (l_i + t_spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i.

verification.spec: Instantiate scheduler with stubbed l_i, t_spec, o_i, t_i^TPOT for a few requests; call A(r) computation routine and assert returned value matches the formula (l_i + t_spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i within 1e-6. Then run SLO-customized selection and assert sum of approximated path probabilities in T_i >= min(A(r_i), d+1) when budget allows.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the decomposition of Theorem 3.1: E[acc(T)] = sum over non-root nodes v in T of f(v), where f(v) is the path probability from root to v.

verification.spec: On a synthetic token tree with known per-edge acceptance probabilities, compute E[acc(T)] both (a) by Monte Carlo simulation of LLM acceptance and (b) via the sum-of-path-probabilities formula. Assert the two values agree within 1e-2 over 10000 simulated trials.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code implements the relaxed TPOT constraint by replacing acc(T_i) with its expectation E[acc(T_i)], as described in the simplification preceding Theorem 3.1.

verification.spec: Inspect the scheduler implementation: assert that the TPOT-satisfaction check uses the sum of approximated path probabilities (an expectation surrogate) rather than a hard count of guaranteed-accepted tokens.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the speculative decoding pipeline produces decoded request output of the form 'An atom consists of three ...' for the prompt 'Describe the structure of an atom.', demonstrating end-to-end correctness of the speculate-select-verify loop on a representative example

verification.spec: Invoke the full AdaServe decoding pipeline with prompt 'Describe the structure of an atom.' on Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct + Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct draft; assert the output begins with a coherent continuation similar to 'An atom consists of three' (allow paraphrase) and that the verification step returns at least one accepted token per iteration on average

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the budget constraint sum_{i=1}^{n} |T_i| <= B is correctly enforced during token tree construction in AdaServe.

verification.spec: Construct multiple token trees via the AdaServe scheduler with budget B=16 over n=4 requests; assert that the sum of |T_i| across all returned trees is <= 16 in all test cases (including edge cases where some requests need more tokens than others).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that for each request r_i, E[acc(T_i)] >= A(r_i) where A(r_i) = (l_i + t_spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i, i.e., the TPOT SLO constraint is enforced in the SLO-customized selection phase.

verification.spec: Set up synthetic requests with known l_i, o_i, t_i^TPOT, t_spec; run AdaServe's SLO-customized selection; assert that for each request the cumulative approximated path probability sum across selected nodes is >= min(A(r_i), d+1) whenever the budget is sufficient.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the relaxed TPOT constraint E[acc(T_i)] >= A(r_i) is used (replacing acc(T_i) with its expectation) in the AdaServe scheduling algorithm.

verification.spec: Inspect the AdaServe scheduler source; assert that the SLO-customized selection phase uses expected acceptance (sum of f(v) approximations) rather than realized acc(T_i) when checking the TPOT constraint.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Theorem 3.1's decomposition E[acc(T)] = sum_{v in T} f(v) holds in the implementation (the expected number of accepted tokens in a tree equals the sum of path probabilities of its nodes).

verification.spec: On a small synthetic token tree with known per-edge acceptance probabilities, compute E[acc(T)] both by Monte Carlo simulation (10000 samples) and by summing path probabilities f(v) across all nodes; assert the two values agree within 2% relative error.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the path probability f(v) for a node v in a token tree is computed as the product of conditional probabilities along the path from the root to v.

verification.spec: Construct a token tree where each edge has known conditional probability; for several nodes v, compute f(v) from the code and assert it equals the product of conditional probabilities along Path(v) within 1e-6.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the optimization objective max sum_{i=1}^{n} sum_{v in T_i} f(v) (reformulated objective using Theorem 3.1) is what the AdaServe scheduler optimizes.

verification.spec: Set up a small synthetic problem with known optimal solution; run the AdaServe selection on it; assert that the total sum of f(v) over selected nodes equals the known optimum (computed via brute force) within 1% relative error.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Figure 5's worked example of SLO-customized speculative decoding is reproduced: with batch=2, budget=8, beam width w=2, depth d=3, the SLO-customized selection adds t1^(0) for r0 (A_cap=0.6) and {t1^(1), t2^(1)} for r1 (A_cap=0.8), and the throughput-optimized phase fills the remaining budget=3 with the top-3 highest-probability remaining tokens.

verification.spec: Reconstruct the exact synthetic scenario in Figure 5 (probabilities and A_cap values given), run AdaServe's SLO-customized + throughput-optimized selection, and assert the resulting draft token trees match the trees shown in Figure 5 token-for-token.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the draft model logits are used to approximate path probabilities: f(v) ≈ M_q(v | X, Path(v.parent)) * f(v.parent).

verification.spec: Run AdaServe with a small draft model on a fixed input; extract the f(v) values used during selection; assert they equal the product of draft-model conditional probabilities along Path(v) within 1e-5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Algorithm 2's SLO-customized selection loop respects the per-request token limit n_max during the SLO-customized selection phase.

verification.spec: Configure a synthetic scenario where A_cap(r_i) cannot be reached even after exhausting the candidate tree; run Algorithm 2 with n_max set explicitly; assert that no request's draft tree receives more than n_max nodes during the SLO-customized phase.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the GetTop operation in Algorithm 2 selects the candidate node with the maximum approximated path probability across (union of T_cand(r_i)) - S_added.

verification.spec: On a synthetic set of candidate trees with known f(v) values, repeatedly call GetTop and assert each call returns the node with maximum f(v) among unselected nodes; assert ordering is strictly decreasing in f(v).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the adaptive depth formula d = clip(D_min, D_max, c1 * B1 / (n * w)) is used to set speculation depth at the beginning of each iteration based on number of active requests n.

verification.spec: Call the AdaServe adaptive controller with varying values of n (e.g., n=1, 4, 16, 64) and fixed B1, c1, w, D_min, D_max; assert the returned d follows the clipped formula and equals expected values within 0; check it decreases as n grows.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the adaptive beam width formula w = clip(1, W_max, c2 * B2 / n) is used to set speculation beam width at the beginning of each iteration.

sub_tasks.requirements: A unit test exists that verifies the n_max parameter enforces the per-request token limit during SLO-customized selection, ensuring no request receives more than n_max tokens in this phase even if its A_cap target has not been met

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic batch with one request whose A_cap requires many low-probability tokens. Run the SLO-customized selection routine with n_max=3. Assert the resulting draft token tree for that request contains at most 3 tokens (plus root), regardless of whether A_cap was reached.
verification.paper_cite: Section 4.3, Step 2 (SLO-customized token selection, n_max constraint)

sub_tasks.requirements: A behavioral test verifies that the throughput-optimized selection step selects tokens by global ranking across all requests (not per-request round-robin), i.e. it picks the top-k tokens by approximated path probability from the union of all candidate trees

verification.spec: Construct two synthetic candidate trees: request A with remaining candidates having probabilities [0.9, 0.85, 0.8] and request B with [0.3, 0.2, 0.1]. With remaining budget=3, run the throughput-optimized selection. Assert that all 3 selected tokens come from request A's tree (the globally highest-probability tokens), not split evenly between requests.

sub_tasks.requirements: A behavioral test verifies that the SLO-customized + throughput-optimized selection pipeline halts selection exactly when the global token budget B reaches 0, and never exceeds B

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic batch of 3 requests with candidate trees each containing 10 nodes. Set total budget B=7. Run the full selection pipeline (SLO-customized then throughput-optimized). Assert that sum of |T_i| across all output draft trees equals exactly 7 (or less only if all candidate trees were exhausted), and never exceeds 7.

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)
Code has been implemented and run such that the SLO constraint formulation requires acc(T_i) >= (l_i + t^spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i for each request as expressed in the paper's constraint equation.

verification.spec: Construct test cases with known (l_i, t^spec, t_i^TPOT, o_i); call the scheduler's A(r_i) computation function; assert returned threshold equals (l_i + t^spec)/t_i^TPOT - o_i.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that A_cap(r) = min(A(r), d+1) caps the required acceptance threshold at d+1 (the maximum verifiable tokens per request given speculation depth d).

verification.spec: For test cases with A(r) > d+1 and A(r) <= d+1, call the A_cap computation; assert it equals min(A(r), d+1).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the algorithm correctly decomposes E[acc(T_i)] = sum_{v in T_i} f(v), the path-probability decomposition theorem.

verification.spec: Construct a small token tree with known path probabilities f(v) at each node; call expected_accepted_tokens(tree); assert it equals sum of f(v) over all non-root nodes, matching Theorem 3.1.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the budget constraint sum_{i=1}^{n} |T_i| <= B is enforced in the token tree construction.

verification.spec: Run AdaServe scheduler with a small budget B and multiple requests; assert that the total number of tokens across all constructed draft token trees never exceeds B.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the adaptive control formula sets speculation depth d = clip(D_min, D_max, c1 * B1/(n*w)) based on active request count n.

verification.spec: Mock the active request count n and parameters (B1, w, c1, D_min, D_max); call the adaptive depth selection function; assert d matches clip(D_min, D_max, c1*B1/(n*w)).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the beam search in the speculation phase uses configurable beam width w and depth d, producing candidate token trees consistent with the paper's algorithm.

verification.spec: Run the speculation phase with a small draft model, d=3, w=2 on a test request; assert the resulting candidate token tree has depth 3 and that all non-root layers contain exactly w nodes per request.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Step 1 (speculation) builds, per request, a candidate token tree of depth d and beam width w by running d parallel draft-model decoding steps and selecting the top-w children by approximated path probability at each level

verification.spec: For a batch of n requests with given d and w, invoke the speculation function and assert: (1) exactly d decoding steps are performed, (2) each non-root level of each candidate tree contains exactly w nodes, (3) selected children at each level correspond to top-w by Mq(v | path) approximated path probability

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Step 2 (SLO-customized selection) greedily inserts top-probability nodes from T_cand(r_i) into T_i until cumulative approximated path-prob >= A_cap(r_i) = min(A(r_i), d+1), capped by per-request limit n_max

verification.spec: On synthetic candidate trees with known f(v), call the SLO-customized selection routine with varied A(r) values and n_max; assert: (1) selection stops when cumulative f sum reaches A_cap(r_i) or |T_i| == n_max, (2) selected nodes are highest-probability available, (3) parents are always present before children

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the throughput-optimized selection step performs a global greedy ranking of all remaining candidate nodes across all requests by approximated path probability, and adds top-scoring nodes to their respective draft token trees until the overall token budget B is exhausted.

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic scenario with 2-3 requests, each with a small candidate token tree of nodes annotated with approximated path probabilities. After running SLO-customized selection (with mock A_cap values), invoke the throughput-optimized selection function with a known remaining budget R. Assert that (a) exactly R nodes are added across all draft trees, (b) the added nodes correspond to the globally top-R remaining candidates ranked by approximated path probability, and (c) each added node's parent is already present in its draft tree (tree validity). Also verify that when budget is 0, no nodes are added.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the verification step submits the per-request draft token trees to the target LLM and verifies all speculated tokens in parallel using tree-based verification (shared-prefix attention mask), returning the set of accepted tokens per request.

verification.spec: Build a small target LLM (e.g., a tiny Llama-style model) and construct a batch of draft token trees with branching structure for 2 requests. Run the verification function and assert: (a) the LLM forward pass is invoked once (not once per path), (b) the attention mask correctly encodes the tree structure so that each token only attends to its ancestors in the tree (verify by checking the mask tensor), (c) the function returns a list of accepted tokens per request consistent with greedy LLM logits along each path, and (d) accepted-path tokens match those produced by a reference sequential verification on the same tree.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that at the start of each decoding iteration, the speculation depth d and beam width w are dynamically computed from the current number of active requests n using the paper's adaptive policy (d clipped between D_min and D_max based on B1/(n*c1), w clipped up to W_max based on B2/(n*c2)).

verification.spec: Instantiate the adaptive controller with fixed D_max, D_min, W_max, B1, B2, c1, c2. For a sweep of n values (e.g., n=1, 4, 16, 64, 256), call the adaptive control function and assert: (a) returned d equals clip(D_min, D_max, floor(B1/(n*c1))) and returned w equals clip(1, W_max, floor(B2/(n*c2))) per the paper's formulas, (b) d and w are monotonically non-increasing as n grows, and (c) at very small n the values saturate at D_max/W_max while at very large n they saturate at D_min/1.
